title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mercimek Corbasi: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make Mercimek Corbasi, a comforting Turkish red lentil soup. This authentic recipe is easy, healthy, and packed with flavor. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup recipe
best Turkish Red Lentil Soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Mercimek Corbasi: Turkish Red Lentil Soup
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?
Ingredients You Need for Mercimek Corbasi
Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi
Serving Suggestions for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Variations and Substitutions for Mercimek Corbasi
seo.imageAlt: A warm bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, traditional Turkish red lentil soup, garnished with fresh mint.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a hearty bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish red lentil soup, perfect for a cozy meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, cherished for its comforting warmth and vibrant flavor. This creamy, hearty soup is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a starter or a light meal. Its simplicity belies a rich taste profile, making it a favorite for all seasons.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear; drain well. In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 1 tablespoon of butter or heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, allowing it to deepen in color and flavor. Add the dried mint, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Stir for about 30 seconds until aromatic, being careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Liquids
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in the vegetable or chicken broth and 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Let the soup simmer gently for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom of the pot. If the soup becomes too thick, you can add a little more water or broth.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili Mint Butter
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-low heat. Add an additional 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or to taste). Cook for about 30 seconds, just until the mint becomes fragrant and the pepper flakes sizzle, being careful not to burn the butter.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the chili mint butter over each serving. Garnish with fresh mint, if desired, and serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing into the soup.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, ensure lentils are cooked until very soft before blending. You can pass the blended soup through a fine-mesh sieve if you prefer a silkier texture.
The chili mint butter (nane yağı) is crucial for authentic flavor; do not skip it! Adjust the red pepper flakes to your spice preference.
Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with a wedge of fresh lemon. Squeezing lemon juice into the hot soup brightens the flavors and adds a zesty finish that truly elevates the dish.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Turkish cuisine with one of its most beloved staples: Mercimek Corbasi, the iconic Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, creamy, and incredibly flavorful soup is more than just an appetizer; it's a testament to the simplicity and richness of Turkish cooking, offering comfort in every spoonful. Whether you've enjoyed it in a bustling Istanbul lokanta or are encountering it for the first time, Mercimek Corbasi promises a delightful culinary journey. Perfect for any season, it’s a nutritious and satisfying dish that’s surprisingly easy to master at home.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-SUH," literally translates to "lentil soup" in Turkish. While there are various lentil soups in Turkish cuisine, the red lentil version is by far the most popular and widely recognized. It's a staple found on almost every restaurant menu, in homes across Turkey, and is often served as a comforting start to any meal, or even as a light meal in itself.

At its heart, Mercimek Corbasi is a velvety smooth soup made primarily from red lentils, simmered with aromatic vegetables like onions and garlic, and often enriched with a touch of tomato paste or red pepper paste. What truly sets it apart, however, is the finishing touch: a sizzling drizzle of butter infused with dried mint and paprika (or Aleppo pepper). This vibrant oil adds a layer of depth, aroma, and visual appeal that transforms a simple lentil soup into something truly special. It’s known for its beautiful orange-red hue, its smooth texture (achieved by blending), and a flavor profile that is both earthy and bright, with a subtle warmth from the spices. This soup embodies the spirit of Turkish hospitality, offering a bowl of warmth and nourishment to all who partake.

## Ingredients You Need for Mercimek Corbasi

Creating an authentic bowl of Mercimek Corbasi requires a few key ingredients, most of which are likely already in your pantry. The beauty of this recipe lies in its simplicity and the way humble ingredients come together to create something extraordinary.

For the Soup Base:
*   1 cup (200g) red lentils, rinsed thoroughly
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   2-3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1 tablespoon olive oil (for sautéing)
*   1 tablespoon tomato paste (domates salçası) or red pepper paste (biber salçası)
*   6 cups (1.5 liters) hot water or vegetable/chicken broth
*   1 teaspoon dried mint (nane)
*   1 teaspoon sweet paprika (tatlı toz biber)
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin (kimyon)
*   Salt and black pepper to taste

For the Mint Butter Drizzle (Nane Yağlı Sos):
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon dried mint (nane)
*   1 teaspoon sweet paprika or hot red pepper flakes (pul biber)

Optional Garnish:
*   Lemon wedges
*   Fresh mint sprigs
*   A pinch of sumac

Ensure your red lentils are well-rinsed to remove any impurities and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. The quality of your tomato or pepper paste also makes a difference, so opt for a good quality Turkish brand if available.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Mercimek Corbasi

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, even for beginner cooks. Follow these steps to create a delicious and authentic Turkish red lentil soup.

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Start by thoroughly rinsing your red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This step is crucial for a clean-tasting soup. Drain well and set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, being careful not to brown them.
3.  **Add Garlic and Paste:** Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Then, add the tomato paste (or red pepper paste) and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Cooking the paste helps to deepen its flavor and remove any raw taste.
4.  **Incorporate Lentils and Spices:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, along with the dried mint, sweet paprika, and cumin. Stir everything together and cook for 1-2 minutes, allowing the spices to toast slightly and release their aromas.
5.  **Simmer the Soup:** Pour in the hot water or broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and mushy. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend to Perfection:** Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, you can carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be cautious with hot liquids – fill only halfway and hold the lid with a towel). Return the blended soup to the pot.
7.  **Season and Adjust:** Return the soup to low heat. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, you can add a little more hot water or broth to reach your desired consistency. Let it gently simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
8.  **Prepare the Mint Butter Drizzle:** While the soup is simmering, melt the unsalted butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the dried mint and sweet paprika (or hot red pepper flakes). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant and the butter turns a vibrant red. Be careful not to burn the spices.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the aromatic mint butter over each serving. Garnish with a lemon wedge and a fresh mint sprig if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving the perfect bowl of Mercimek Corbasi is an art that benefits from a few expert touches. Here are some pro tips to elevate your soup from good to unforgettable:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils Thoroughly:** This cannot be stressed enough. Rinsing red lentils until the water runs completely clear removes excess starch, which can make your soup cloudy and sometimes give it a slightly gummy texture. A clear rinse ensures a vibrant color and smooth consistency.
*   **Sauté Aromatics Properly:** Don't rush the sautéing of onions and garlic. Cooking them slowly until they are translucent and fragrant builds a crucial flavor base for the soup. Avoid browning them too much, as this can impart a bitter taste.
*   **Cook the Tomato/Pepper Paste:** Always cook your tomato or red pepper paste for a couple of minutes after adding it to the pot. This step, known as "cooking off" the paste, deepens its flavor, sweetens it, and removes any raw, metallic notes.
*   **Use Hot Broth/Water:** Adding hot water or broth to your simmering aromatics and lentils helps maintain the temperature of the pot and ensures a more even cooking process.
*   **Blend Until Velvety Smooth:** The signature texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its creaminess. Use an immersion blender and blend until there are absolutely no visible pieces of lentil or onion. For an extra luxurious texture, you can even pass the blended soup through a fine-mesh sieve, though this is usually unnecessary with a good blender.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Mint Butter Drizzle:** The *nane yağlı sos* (mint butter sauce) is not just a garnish; it's an integral part of the Mercimek Corbasi experience. The vibrant flavors of dried mint and paprika, toasted in butter, add an incomparable aroma, taste, and visual appeal. Make it fresh just before serving for maximum impact.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb liquid as they cool. If your soup becomes too thick after blending or reheating, simply thin it out with a little extra hot water or broth until it reaches your preferred consistency.
*   **Taste and Adjust Seasoning:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt and pepper as needed. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the end can brighten all the flavors.

## Serving Suggestions for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is a versatile soup that can be enjoyed in many ways, but there are traditional accompaniments that truly enhance the experience.

*   **Lemon Wedges:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice is non-negotiable. The bright acidity cuts through the richness of the lentils and butter, awakening all the flavors and adding a delightful tang. Always serve with plenty of lemon wedges on the side.
*   **Crusty Bread:** Turkish red lentil soup is almost always served with fresh, crusty bread, such as a simple white loaf or traditional Turkish pide. It's perfect for soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Chili Flakes (Pul Biber):** For those who enjoy a bit of heat, a sprinkle of extra Turkish red pepper flakes (pul biber) on top is a popular addition.
*   **Sumac:** A tiny pinch of sumac sprinkled over the soup adds another layer of subtle tartness and a beautiful reddish hue, complementing the lemon.
*   **Fresh Mint:** A few fresh mint leaves torn and sprinkled over the top can add a burst of freshness and a lovely aromatic contrast.
*   **As an Appetizer:** Traditionally, Mercimek Corbasi is served as a starter to a larger Turkish meal, often alongside a selection of mezes.
*   **Light Meal:** Given its hearty and nutritious nature, a generous bowl of this red lentil soup with some bread makes for a satisfying and comforting light lunch or dinner.

## Variations and Substitutions for Mercimek Corbasi

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi is perfect as is, there's always room for a little creativity in the kitchen. Here are some variations and substitutions you can explore:

*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and texture, you can add finely diced carrots, potatoes, or celery along with the onions. Cook them until soft before blending. A small amount of sweet red bell pepper can also add a nice flavor dimension.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth to the soup, complementing the earthy lentils beautifully.
*   **Spice it Up:** If you prefer a spicier soup, increase the amount of red pepper flakes in the butter drizzle or add a pinch of cayenne pepper to the soup base.
*   **Herbal Notes:** While dried mint is traditional, you can experiment with other dried herbs like oregano (kekik) or a bay leaf added during the simmering process (remember to remove the bay leaf before blending).
*   **Vegan Option:** To make Mercimek Corbasi completely vegan, simply substitute the butter in the finishing drizzle with olive oil. The flavor profile will be slightly different but still delicious. Ensure your broth is vegetable-based.
*   **Creamier Texture:** For an even richer and creamier soup, some recipes call for a splash of milk or cream stirred in at the very end, just before serving. However, this is not traditional and often unnecessary given the natural creaminess of blended red lentils.
*   **Broth Choice:** While water works perfectly fine, using a good quality chicken or vegetable broth can add an extra layer of savory depth to the soup.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary hug in a bowl, a staple that embodies the warmth and hospitality of Turkish culture. Its simple ingredients, combined with thoughtful preparation, result in a dish that is both nourishing and deeply satisfying. Whether you're seeking a comforting meal on a cold day or a flavorful introduction to Turkish cuisine, this red lentil soup is a perfect choice. Give this recipe a try, and bring a taste of Turkey into your home!
